```json
        "contents": "const fs = require('fs');\n\nconst CONTEXT_FILE = '.cursor/rules/architecture.md';\n\nasync function main() {\n  const input = JSON.parse(await readStdin());\n  \n  if (input.status !== 'completed' || input.loop_count \u003e= 1) {\n    console.log(JSON.stringify({}));\n    return;\n  }\n  \n  // Only trigger if codebase-context.md was NOT the only file changed\n  console.log(JSON.stringify({\n    followup_message: &#96;Only go ahead if you made code changes (not just updated ${CONTEXT_FILE}):\n    \n    Update ${CONTEXT_FILE} with any new information about the codebase from the changes you just made.       "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "---\ndescription: \"Design guidelines and principles for the project\"\nalwaysApply: true\n---\n\n## General Principles\n\n### Cursor \u0026 Interaction\n\n- **All interactive elements** (buttons, tabs, select dropdowns, clickable items) must have &#96;cursor-pointer&#96; class\n- Disabled elements should use &#96;cursor-not-allowed&#96; with &#96;disabled:opacity-50&#96;\n- Hover states should be smooth with &#96;transition-colors&#96; or &#96;transition-opacity&#96;\n\n### Typography\n\n- **Font sizes**: Use &#96;text-[13px]&#96; for most UI elements, &#96;text-[14px]&#96; for primary buttons, &#96;text-[15px]&#96; for headings/subheadings, &#96;text-[12px]&#96; for labels/secondary text, &#96;text-[18px]&#96; for large metric values\n- **Font weights**: &#96;font-medium&#96; for most text, &#96;font-semibold&#96; for headings and primary buttons\n- **Text colors**: Use semantic color tokens (&#96;text-foreground&#96;, &#96;text-muted-foreground&#96;, &#96;text-background&#96;)\n\n### Spacing\n\n- Use consistent spacing scale: &#96;space-y-2&#96;, &#96;space-y-4&#96;, &#96;space-y-6&#96; for vertical spacing\n- Padding: &#96;p-4&#96; for cards/containers, &#96;px-4 py-3&#96; for table cells, &#96;px-8&#96; for primary buttons\n- Gaps: &#96;gap-2&#96;, &#96;gap-3&#96;, &#96;gap-4&#96; for flex/grid layouts\n\n## Component Patterns\n\n### Buttons\n\n#### Primary Button\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\nclassName =\n  \"h-11 px-8 rounded-md text-[14px] font-semibold bg-foreground text-background hover:opacity-90 transition-opacity cursor-pointer flex items-center gap-2 shadow-sm\";\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- Height: &#96;h-11&#96;\n- Padding: &#96;px-8&#96;\n- Background: &#96;bg-foreground&#96; with &#96;text-background&#96;\n- Hover: &#96;hover:opacity-90&#96;\n\n#### Secondary Button\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\nclassName =\n  \"h-10 px-4 rounded-md text-[13px] font-medium border border-border bg-background hover:bg-accent transition-colors flex items-center gap-2 cursor-pointer\";\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- Height: &#96;h-10&#96;\n- Border: &#96;border border-border&#96;\n- Hover: &#96;hover:bg-accent&#96;\n\n#### Icon Button\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\nclassName =\n  \"flex-shrink-0 w-10 h-10 rounded-md text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground hover:bg-accent transition-colors flex items-center justify-center cursor-pointer\";\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- Square: &#96;w-10 h-10&#96;\n",

        "originalContents": "---\ndescription: \"Design guidelines and principles for the project\"\nalwaysApply: true\n---\n\n## General Principles\n\n### Cursor \u0026 Interaction\n\n- **All interactive elements** (buttons, tabs, select dropdowns, clickable items) must have &#96;cursor-pointer&#96; class\n- Disabled elements should use &#96;cursor-not-allowed&#96; with &#96;disabled:opacity-50&#96;\n- Hover states should be smooth with &#96;transition-colors&#96; or &#96;transition-opacity&#96;\n\n### Typography\n\n- **Font sizes**: Use &#96;text-[13px]&#96; for most UI elements, &#96;text-[14px]&#96; for primary buttons, &#96;text-[15px]&#96; for headings/subheadings, &#96;text-[12px]&#96; for labels/secondary text, &#96;text-[18px]&#96; for large metric values\n- **Font weights**: &#96;font-medium&#96; for most text, &#96;font-semibold&#96; for headings and primary buttons\n- **Text colors**: Use semantic color tokens (&#96;text-foreground&#96;, &#96;text-muted-foreground&#96;, &#96;text-background&#96;)\n\n### Spacing\n\n- Use consistent spacing scale: &#96;space-y-2&#96;, &#96;space-y-4&#96;, &#96;space-y-6&#96; for vertical spacing\n- Padding: &#96;p-4&#96; for cards/containers, &#96;px-4 py-3&#96; for table cells, &#96;px-8&#96; for primary buttons\n- Gaps: &#96;gap-2&#96;, &#96;gap-3&#96;, &#96;gap-4&#96; for flex/grid layouts\n\n## Component Patterns\n\n### Buttons\n\n#### Primary Button\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\nclassName =\n  \"h-11 px-8 rounded-md text-[14px] font-semibold bg-foreground text-background hover:opacity-90 transition-opacity cursor-pointer flex items-center gap-2 shadow-sm\";\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- Height: &#96;h-11&#96;\n- Padding: &#96;px-8&#96;\n- Background: &#96;bg-foreground&#96; with &#96;text-background&#96;\n- Hover: &#96;hover:opacity-90&#96;\n\n#### Secondary Button\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\nclassName =\n  \"h-10 px-4 rounded-md text-[13px] font-medium border border-border bg-background hover:bg-accent transition-colors flex items-center gap-2 cursor-pointer\";\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- Height: &#96;h-10&#96;\n- Border: &#96;border border-border&#96;\n- Hover: &#96;hover:bg-accent&#96;\n\n#### Icon Button\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\nclassName =\n  \"flex-shrink-0 w-10 h-10 rounded-md text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground hover:bg-accent transition-colors flex items-center justify-center cursor-pointer\";\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- Square: &#96;w-10 h-10&#96;\n",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 62
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".cursor/rules/design.md"
      },
      "score": 0.2113063931465149

        "originalContents": "{\n  \"params\": {\n    \"model\": \"gpt-4.1\" // the name of the openai model\n  },\n  \"system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful assistant...\", // the system prompt for the LLM.\n  \"tools\": [\n    // array of tools that the LLM can call. Each tool should have:\n    {\n      \"type\": \"client\", // the type of the tool.         "contents": "- Centered icon with flex\n\n#### Dashed Border Button (Add/Secondary Actions)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\nclassName =\n  \"w-full h-10 px-4 rounded-md text-[13px] font-medium border border-dashed border-border bg-muted/20 hover:bg-muted/40 transition-colors flex items-center justify-center gap-2 text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground cursor-pointer\";\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Tabs\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\nclassName={&#96;px-4 py-2 text-[13px] font-medium border-b-2 transition-colors cursor-pointer ${\n  active ? \"border-foreground text-foreground\" : \"border-transparent text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground\"\n}&#96;}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- Active: &#96;border-foreground text-foreground&#96;\n- Inactive: &#96;border-transparent text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground&#96;\n- Always include &#96;cursor-pointer&#96;\n\n### Form Elements\n\n#### Text Input\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\nclassName =\n  \"w-full h-10 px-3 rounded-md text-[13px] border border-border bg-background focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-accent focus:border-transparent\";\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- Height: &#96;h-10&#96;\n- Focus: &#96;focus:ring-2 focus:ring-accent&#96;\n\n#### Select Dropdown\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\nclassName =\n  \"h-10 px-4 pr-10 rounded-md text-[13px] border border-border bg-background text-foreground focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-accent focus:border-transparent cursor-pointer appearance-none min-w-[140px]\";\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- Custom dropdown arrow with absolute positioning\n- &#96;appearance-none&#96; to hide default arrow\n- &#96;cursor-pointer&#96; required\n\n### Cards \u0026 Containers\n\n#### Card Container\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\nclassName = \"border rounded-xl p-4 bg-muted/10\";\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- Border radius: &#96;rounded-xl&#96;\n- Background: &#96;bg-muted/10&#96; for subtle background\n\n#### Table Container\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\nclassName = \"border rounded-xl overflow-hidden\";\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- &#96;overflow-hidden&#96; to clip table borders\n\n### Tables\n\n#### Table Header\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\nclassName = \"bg-muted/50 border-b border-border\";\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- Background: &#96;bg-muted/50&#96;\n- Border: &#96;border-b border-border&#96;\n\n#### Table Cell\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;tsx\nclassName = \"px-4 py-3 text-[13px] text-foreground\";\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- Padding: &#96;px-4 py-3&#96;\n- Font size: &#96;text-[13px]&#96;\n\n",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\nfunction readStdin() {\n  return new Promise(resolve =\u003e {\n    let data = '';\n    process.stdin.on('data', chunk =\u003e data += chunk);\n    process.stdin.on('end', () =\u003e resolve(data));\n  });\n}",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "async function main() {"

            "lineNumber": 14

            "text": "}"

            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "function readStdin() {"

          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "  return new Promise(resolve =\u003e {"
          },

            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "    let data = '';"

        "originalContents": "\n\nfunction readStdin() {\n  return new Promise(resolve =\u003e {\n    let data = '';\n    process.stdin.on('data', chunk =\u003e data += chunk);\n    process.stdin.on('end', () =\u003e resolve(data));\n  });\n}",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "- Tab navigation aligned with Evaluate button padding\n\n### Provider Selection\n\n- Toggle buttons with checkmark icons when selected\n- Selected: &#96;bg-foreground text-background border-foreground&#96;\n- Unselected: &#96;bg-background text-muted-foreground border-border hover:bg-accent/50&#96;\n\n### File Upload\n\n- Hidden file input with custom button\n- Loading state with spinner\n- Success state with green checkmark icon\n- File name truncation at 30 characters\n\n### Results Tables\n\n- Row numbers (1, 2, 3...) instead of IDs in per-provider results\n- Full width tables with proper column alignment\n- Alternating row styling with borders\n\n## Accessibility\n\n- All buttons should have appropriate &#96;aria-label&#96; when icon-only\n- Focus states should be visible (&#96;focus:ring-2 focus:ring-accent&#96;)\n- Disabled states should be clearly indicated\n\n## Animation \u0026 Transitions\n\n- Use &#96;transition-colors&#96; for color changes\n- Use &#96;transition-opacity&#96; for opacity changes\n- Spinner animations: &#96;animate-spin&#96;\n- Smooth scrolling: &#96;behavior: \"smooth\"&#96; for programmatic scrolling\n",

        "originalContents": "- Tab navigation aligned with Evaluate button padding\n\n### Provider Selection\n\n- Toggle buttons with checkmark icons when selected\n- Selected: &#96;bg-foreground text-background border-foreground&#96;\n- Unselected: &#96;bg-background text-muted-foreground border-border hover:bg-accent/50&#96;\n\n### File Upload\n\n- Hidden file input with custom button\n- Loading state with spinner\n- Success state with green checkmark icon\n- File name truncation at 30 characters\n\n### Results Tables\n\n- Row numbers (1, 2, 3...) instead of IDs in per-provider results\n- Full width tables with proper column alignment\n- Alternating row styling with borders\n\n## Accessibility\n\n- All buttons should have appropriate &#96;aria-label&#96; when icon-only\n- Focus states should be visible (&#96;focus:ring-2 focus:ring-accent&#96;)\n- Disabled states should be clearly indicated\n\n## Animation \u0026 Transitions\n\n- Use &#96;transition-colors&#96; for color changes\n- Use &#96;transition-opacity&#96; for opacity changes\n- Spinner animations: &#96;animate-spin&#96;\n- Smooth scrolling: &#96;behavior: \"smooth\"&#96; for programmatic scrolling\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "| &#96;stt-eval&#96;      | &#96;jobs&#96;            | STT provider evaluation   |\n| &#96;tts-eval&#96;      | &#96;jobs&#96;            | TTS provider evaluation   |\n| &#96;llm-unit-test&#96; | &#96;agent_test_jobs&#96; | Agent unit test execution |\n| &#96;llm-benchmark&#96; | &#96;agent_test_jobs&#96; | Multi-model benchmarking  |\n| &#96;chat&#96;          | &#96;simulation_jobs&#96; | Chat-based simulation     |\n| &#96;voice&#96;         | &#96;simulation_jobs&#96; | Voice-based simulation    |\n\n### Job Lifecycle\n\n1. **Creation**: Job created with &#96;in_progress&#96; status and &#96;details&#96; JSON containing recovery info\n2. **Execution**: Background thread runs the task\n3. **Completion**: Job updated with &#96;done&#96; status and &#96;results&#96; JSON\n4. **Recovery**: On app startup, &#96;job_recovery.py&#96; restarts any &#96;in_progress&#96; jobs\n\n### Job Status Values\n\n- &#96;in_progress&#96; - Job is running\n- &#96;done&#96; - Job completed (check &#96;results.error&#96; for failure)\n- &#96;cancelled&#96; - Job was cancelled (not currently used)\n\n---\n\n## External Integrations\n\n### Pense CLI\n\nThe backend orchestrates the &#96;pense&#96; CLI tool for actual evaluations:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# STT Evaluation\npense stt eval -p \u003cprovider\u003e -l \u003clanguage\u003e -i \u003cinput_dir\u003e -o \u003coutput_dir\u003e\npense stt leaderboard -o \u003coutput_dir\u003e -s \u003csummary_dir\u003e\n\n# TTS Evaluation\npense tts eval -p \u003cprovider\u003e -l \u003clanguage\u003e -i \u003cinput_csv\u003e -o \u003coutput_dir\u003e\npense tts leaderboard -o \u003coutput_dir\u003e -s \u003csummary_dir\u003e\n\n# LLM Tests\npense llm tests run -c \u003cconfig.json\u003e -o \u003coutput_dir\u003e -m \u003cmodel\u003e\npense llm tests leaderboard -o \u003coutput_dir\u003e -s \u003csummary_dir\u003e\n\n# LLM Simulations\npense llm simulations run -c \u003cconfig.json\u003e -o \u003coutput_dir\u003e -m \u003cmodel\u003e\n\n# Voice Agent Simulation\npense agent simulation -c \u003cconfig.json\u003e -o \u003coutput_dir\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### AWS S3\n\nUsed for:\n\n- Storing input audio files (STT evaluation)\n- Storing generated audio files (TTS evaluation, voice simulations)\n- Storing evaluation results (JSON, CSV, Excel files)\n- Presigned URLs for secure file access\n\nRequired environment variables:\n\n- &#96;S3_OUTPUT_BUCKET&#96; - Target bucket for outputs\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 188,
            "text": "| --------------- | ----------------- | ------------------------- |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 189,
            "text": "| &#96;stt-eval&#96;      | &#96;jobs&#96;            | STT provider evaluation   |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 190,
            "text": "| &#96;tts-eval&#96;      | &#96;jobs&#96;            | TTS provider evaluation   |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 191,
            "text": "| &#96;llm-unit-test&#96; | &#96;agent_test_jobs&#96; | Agent unit test execution |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 192,
            "text": "| &#96;llm-benchmark&#96; | &#96;agent_test_jobs&#96; | Multi-model benchmarking  |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 193,
            "text": "| &#96;chat&#96;          | &#96;simulation_jobs&#96; | Chat-based simulation     |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 194,
            "text": "| &#96;voice&#96;         | &#96;simulation_jobs&#96; | Voice-based simulation    |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 195
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 196,
            "text": "### Job Lifecycle"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 197
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 198,
            "text": "1. **Creation**: Job created with &#96;in_progress&#96; status and &#96;details&#96; JSON containing recovery info"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 199,
            "text": "2. **Execution**: Background thread runs the task"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 200,
            "text": "3. **Completion**: Job updated with &#96;done&#96; status and &#96;results&#96; JSON"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 201,
            "text": "4. **Recovery**: On app startup, &#96;job_recovery.py&#96; restarts any &#96;in_progress&#96; jobs"
          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 203,
            "text": "### Job Status Values"

            "text": "- &#96;AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID&#96; / &#96;AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY&#96; (optional, falls back to IAM role)"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "| &#96;stt-eval&#96;      | &#96;jobs&#96;            | STT provider evaluation   |\n| &#96;tts-eval&#96;      | &#96;jobs&#96;            | TTS provider evaluation   |\n| &#96;llm-unit-test&#96; | &#96;agent_test_jobs&#96; | Agent unit test execution |\n| &#96;llm-benchmark&#96; | &#96;agent_test_jobs&#96; | Multi-model benchmarking  |\n| &#96;chat&#96;          | &#96;simulation_jobs&#96; | Chat-based simulation     |\n| &#96;voice&#96;         | &#96;simulation_jobs&#96; | Voice-based simulation    |\n\n### Job Lifecycle\n\n1. **Creation**: Job created with &#96;in_progress&#96; status and &#96;details&#96; JSON containing recovery info\n2. **Execution**: Background thread runs the task\n3. **Completion**: Job updated with &#96;done&#96; status and &#96;results&#96; JSON\n4. **Recovery**: On app startup, &#96;job_recovery.py&#96; restarts any &#96;in_progress&#96; jobs\n\n### Job Status Values\n\n- &#96;in_progress&#96; - Job is running\n- &#96;done&#96; - Job completed (check &#96;results.error&#96; for failure)\n- &#96;cancelled&#96; - Job was cancelled (not currently used)\n\n---\n\n## External Integrations\n\n### Pense CLI\n\nThe backend orchestrates the &#96;pense&#96; CLI tool for actual evaluations:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# STT Evaluation\npense stt eval -p \u003cprovider\u003e -l \u003clanguage\u003e -i \u003cinput_dir\u003e -o \u003coutput_dir\u003e\npense stt leaderboard -o \u003coutput_dir\u003e -s \u003csummary_dir\u003e\n\n# TTS Evaluation\npense tts eval -p \u003cprovider\u003e -l \u003clanguage\u003e -i \u003cinput_csv\u003e -o \u003coutput_dir\u003e\npense tts leaderboard -o \u003coutput_dir\u003e -s \u003csummary_dir\u003e\n\n# LLM Tests\npense llm tests run -c \u003cconfig.json\u003e -o \u003coutput_dir\u003e -m \u003cmodel\u003e\npense llm tests leaderboard -o \u003coutput_dir\u003e -s \u003csummary_dir\u003e\n\n# LLM Simulations\npense llm simulations run -c \u003cconfig.json\u003e -o \u003coutput_dir\u003e -m \u003cmodel\u003e\n\n# Voice Agent Simulation\npense agent simulation -c \u003cconfig.json\u003e -o \u003coutput_dir\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### AWS S3\n\nUsed for:\n\n- Storing input audio files (STT evaluation)\n- Storing generated audio files (TTS evaluation, voice simulations)\n- Storing evaluation results (JSON, CSV, Excel files)\n- Presigned URLs for secure file access\n\nRequired environment variables:\n\n- &#96;S3_OUTPUT_BUCKET&#96; - Target bucket for outputs\n",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 249
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 187
          }
        },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "- &#96;AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID&#96; / &#96;AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY&#96; (optional, falls back to IAM role)\n- &#96;AWS_REGION&#96; - Default: &#96;ap-south-1&#96;\n\n### Supported Providers\n\n**STT Providers**: deepgram, openai, sarvam, google, etc.\n\n**TTS Providers**: smallest, cartesia, openai, google, elevenlabs, etc.\n\n**LLM Providers**: OpenAI, Groq, OpenRouter, Google (via respective API keys)\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Environment Variables\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Database\nDB_ROOT_DIR=/appdata/db          # Directory containing pense.db\n\n# CORS\nCORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=*           # Comma-separated origins (e.g., \"http://localhost:3000,https://app.example.com\")\n\n# JWT Authentication\nJWT_SECRET_KEY=your-secret-key   # REQUIRED: At least 32 characters, change in production!\nJWT_EXPIRATION_HOURS=168         # Token validity (default: 7 days)\n\n# AWS\nS3_OUTPUT_BUCKET=your-bucket\nAWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=xxx\nAWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=xxx\nAWS_REGION=ap-south-1\n\n# Authentication\nGOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=xxx.apps.googleusercontent.com\n\n# Provider API Keys\nOPENAI_API_KEY=xxx\nDEEPGRAM_API_KEY=xxx\nCARTESIA_API_KEY=xxx\nSMALLEST_API_KEY=xxx\nGROQ_API_KEY=xxx\nGOOGLE_API_KEY=xxx\nGOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=/path/to/credentials.json\nSARVAM_API_KEY=xxx\nELEVENLABS_API_KEY=xxx\nOPENROUTER_API_KEY=xxx\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Agent Configuration Schema\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful assistant...\",\n  \"llm\": {\n    \"provider\": \"openai\",\n    \"model\": \"gpt-4.1\"\n  },\n  \"stt\": {\n    \"provider\": \"deepgram\",\n    \"model\": \"nova-2\"\n  },\n  \"tts\": {\n    \"provider\": \"elevenlabs\",\n    \"voice_id\": \"xxx\"\n  },\n  \"speaks_first\": true\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Test Case Configuration Schema\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"input\": \"What's the weather in Mumbai?\",\n  \"evaluation\": {\n    \"type\": \"tool_call\",\n    \"tool_calls\": [\n      {\n        \"tool\": \"get_weather\",\n        \"arguments\": { \"city\": \"Mumbai\" },\n        \"accept_any_arguments\": false\n      }\n    ]\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Persona Configuration Schema\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"gender\": \"female\",\n  \"language\": \"english\",\n  \"interruption_sensitivity\": \"medium\"\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n",

Directory containing pense.db\n\n# CORS\nCORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=*           # Comma-separated origins (e.g., \"http://localhost:3000,https://app.example.com\")\n\n# JWT Authentication\nJWT_SECRET_KEY=your-secret-key   # REQUIRED: At least 32 characters, change in production!\nJWT_EXPIRATION_HOURS=168         # Token validity (default: 7 days)\n\n# AWS\nS3_OUTPUT_BUCKET=your-bucket\nAWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=xxx\nAWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=xxx\nAWS_REGION=ap-south-1\n\n# Authentication\nGOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=xxx.apps.googleusercontent.com\n\n# Provider API Keys\nOPENAI_API_KEY=xxx\nDEEPGRAM_API_KEY=xxx\nCARTESIA_API_KEY=xxx\nSMALLEST_API_KEY=xxx\nGROQ_API_KEY=xxx\nGOOGLE_API_KEY=xxx\nGOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=/path/to/credentials.json\nSARVAM_API_KEY=xxx\nELEVENLABS_API_KEY=xxx\nOPENROUTER_API_KEY=xxx\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Agent Configuration Schema\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful assistant...\",\n  \"llm\": {\n    \"provider\": \"openai\",\n    \"model\": \"gpt-4.1\"\n  },\n  \"stt\": {\n    \"provider\": \"deepgram\",\n    \"model\": \"nova-2\"\n  },\n  \"tts\": {\n    \"provider\": \"elevenlabs\",\n    \"voice_id\": \"xxx\"\n  },\n  \"speaks_first\": true\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Test Case Configuration Schema\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"input\": \"What's the weather in Mumbai?\",\n  \"evaluation\": {\n    \"type\": \"tool_call\",\n    \"tool_calls\": [\n      {\n        \"tool\": \"get_weather\",\n        \"arguments\": { \"city\": \"Mumbai\" },\n        \"accept_any_arguments\": false\n      }\n    ]\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Persona Configuration Schema\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"gender\": \"female\",\n  \"language\": \"english\",\n  \"interruption_sensitivity\": \"medium\"\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "simulations\n  ├── simulation_personas (many-to-many with personas)\n  ├── simulation_scenarios (many-to-many with scenarios)\n  ├── simulation_metrics (many-to-many with metrics)\n  └── simulation_jobs\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Core Tables\n\n| Table             | Purpose                                                                |\n| ----------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| &#96;users&#96;           | User accounts (Google OAuth)                                           |\n| &#96;agents&#96;          | AI agent configurations (system prompt, LLM config, STT/TTS settings)  |\n| &#96;tools&#96;           | Tool/function definitions for agents                                   |\n| &#96;tests&#96;           | Test cases with evaluation criteria                                    |\n| &#96;personas&#96;        | Simulated user personas (characteristics, gender, language)            |\n| &#96;scenarios&#96;       | Conversation scenarios/contexts                                        |\n| &#96;metrics&#96;         | Evaluation criteria for simulations                                    |\n| &#96;simulations&#96;     | Simulation configurations linking agents, personas, scenarios, metrics |\n| &#96;jobs&#96;            | Generic STT/TTS evaluation jobs                                        |\n| &#96;agent_test_jobs&#96; | LLM unit test and benchmark jobs                                       |\n| &#96;simulation_jobs&#96; | Chat/voice simulation jobs                                             |\n\n### Design Patterns\n\n1. **Soft Deletes**: All entity tables use &#96;deleted_at&#96; timestamp for soft deletion\n2. **UUIDs**: All entities use UUID as primary identifier (separate from auto-increment id)\n3. **JSON Config**: Complex configurations stored as JSON strings in &#96;config&#96; columns\n4. **Pivot Tables**: Many-to-many relationships use dedicated pivot tables with soft delete support\n\n---\n\n## API Architecture\n\n### Router Organization\n\nEach router follows a consistent pattern:\n\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 85,
            "text": "  └── simulations (agent_id FK)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87,
            "text": "simulations"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "  ├── simulation_personas (many-to-many with personas)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89,
            "text": "  ├── simulation_scenarios (many-to-many with scenarios)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "  ├── simulation_metrics (many-to-many with metrics)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91,
            "text": "  └── simulation_jobs"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94,
            "text": "### Core Tables"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "| Table             | Purpose                                                                |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "| ----------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "text": "| &#96;users&#96;           | User accounts (Google OAuth)                                           |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "| &#96;agents&#96;          | AI agent configurations (system prompt, LLM config, STT/TTS settings)  |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "| &#96;tools&#96;           | Tool/function definitions for agents                                   |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "| &#96;tests&#96;           | Test cases with evaluation criteria                                    |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "| &#96;personas&#96;        | Simulated user personas (characteristics, gender, language)            |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "| &#96;scenarios&#96;       | Conversation scenarios/contexts                                        |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "text": "| &#96;metrics&#96;         | Evaluation criteria for simulations                                    |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105,
            "text": "| &#96;simulations&#96;     | Simulation configurations linking agents, personas, scenarios, metrics |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "| &#96;jobs&#96;            | Generic STT/TTS evaluation jobs                                        |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107,
            "text": "| &#96;agent_test_jobs&#96; | LLM unit test and benchmark jobs                                       |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "| &#96;simulation_jobs&#96; | Chat/voice simulation jobs                                             |"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110,
            "text": "### Design Patterns"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112,
            "text": "1. **Soft Deletes**: All entity tables use &#96;deleted_at&#96; timestamp for soft deletion"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113,
            "text": "2. **UUIDs**: All entities use UUID as primary identifier (separate from auto-increment id)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "3. **JSON Config**: Complex configurations stored as JSON strings in &#96;config&#96; columns"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "4. **Pivot Tables**: Many-to-many relationships use dedicated pivot tables with soft delete support"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "## API Architecture"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "### Router Organization"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123,
            "text": "Each router follows a consistent pattern:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125,
            "text": "- Pydantic models for request/response validation"
          }

        "originalContents": "simulations\n  ├── simulation_personas (many-to-many with personas)\n  ├── simulation_scenarios (many-to-many with scenarios)\n  ├── simulation_metrics (many-to-many with metrics)\n  └── simulation_jobs\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Core Tables\n\n| Table             | Purpose                                                                |\n| ----------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| &#96;users&#96;           | User accounts (Google OAuth)                                           |\n| &#96;agents&#96;          | AI agent configurations (system prompt, LLM config, STT/TTS settings)  |\n| &#96;tools&#96;         | Evaluation criteria for simulations                                    |\n| &#96;simulations&#96;     | Simulation configurations linking agents, personas, scenarios, metrics |\n| &#96;jobs&#96;            | Generic STT/TTS evaluation jobs                                        |\n| &#96;agent_test_jobs&#96; | LLM unit test and benchmark jobs                                       |\n| &#96;simulation_jobs&#96; | Chat/voice simulation jobs                                             |\n\n### Design Patterns\n\n1. **Soft Deletes**: All entity tables use &#96;deleted_at&#96; timestamp for soft deletion\n2. **UUIDs**: All entities use UUID as primary identifier (separate from auto-increment id)\n3. **JSON Config**: Complex configurations stored as JSON strings in &#96;config&#96; _**Agent (composer-1)**_
```


Updated the hook to run only when `.py` files change.

1. **Checks for changed files**: Reads from `input.changed_files` or `input.files` (handles both string paths and objects with a `path` property).
2. **Filters for `.py` files**: Verifies at least one changed file ends with `.py`.
3. **Early return**: If no `.py` files changed, returns empty JSON and exits.

The hook now triggers only when Python files are modified. The `hooks.json` file doesn't need changes since it only references the script.

The updated logic:
- Keeps the existing status/loop_count checks
- Adds a `.py` file filter
- Handles different input structures for changed files
- Returns early if no `.py` files were changed

---

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Fixed missing logging in `stt.py` and `tts.py`. In `simulations.py`, stdout and stderr are logged after the subprocess call, but in `stt.py` and `tts.py` they were captured but not logged.

Changes:
1. **`stt.py`**: Store the subprocess result and log stdout/stderr after the STT eval command completes
2. **`tts.py`**: Store the subprocess result and log stdout/stderr after the TTS eval command completes

Both now match the pattern in `simulations.py`, so pense command output will appear in backend logs. The logs include the run_id and provider name for easier debugging.

---

_**User (2026-01-15 05:41:15Z)**_

run the subprocess for pense agent simulation properly - that underneath works as follows: 

Parallelization Summary:
Semaphore-based concurrency control: A asyncio.Semaphore(args.parallel) limits how many simulations run concurrently (controlled by -n/--parallel CLI argument).
Task creation: All simulation tasks are created upfront using asyncio.create_task() for each persona × scenario combination. This schedules them all immediately.
Port management: Each parallel simulation gets a unique port. A shared assigned_ports set with an asyncio.Lock prevents port conflicts. Ports are released in a finally block after each simulation completes.
Execution: asyncio.gather(*tasks) waits for all tasks. The semaphore inside run_single_simulation_task() ensures only N simulations run simultaneously.
Subprocess compatibility: When run as a subprocess (e.g., from FastAPI), signal handling is configured to prevent parent process signals from interrupting child simulations, and a fresh event loop is created.



---

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Updated both `_run_pense_chat_simulation` and `_run_pense_voice_simulation` to use `subprocess.Popen` with proper configuration.

**Changes:**

1. **`start_new_session=True`**: Prevents FastAPI signals from reaching the subprocess. Important for the voice simulation that uses async operations with semaphore-based concurrency control.

2. **`env={**os.environ}`**: Passes all environment variables (including API keys and configuration) to the subprocess.

3. **`communicate()`**: Waits for the process to complete and retrieves stdout/stderr.

4. **Compatibility**: Created a result-like object to maintain compatibility with the existing code that checks `result.returncode`.

This ensures:
- The subprocess runs in its own session, isolated from FastAPI signal handling
- Environment variables are available to the pense command
- Async operations inside pense (with semaphore-based concurrency) run without interference
- Output is properly captured and logged

The voice simulation should now run correctly with its internal async operations and parallelization.

<details><summary>Tool use: **search_replace** • Edit file: .cursor/rules/architecture.md</summary>

**Chunk 1**
Lines added: 16, lines removed: 2

```diff
@@ -229,9 +229,23 @@
  
  # LLM Simulations
- pense llm simulations run -c &lt;config.json&gt; -o &lt;output_dir&gt; -m &lt;model&gt;
+ pense llm simulations run -c &lt;config.json&gt; -o &lt;output_dir&gt; -m &lt;model&gt; -n &lt;parallel&gt;
  
  # Voice Agent Simulation
- pense agent simulation -c &lt;config.json&gt; -o &lt;output_dir&gt;
+ pense agent simulation -c &lt;config.json&gt; -o &lt;output_dir&gt; -n &lt;parallel&gt;
  &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Subprocess Execution Pattern**: The backend uses &#96;subprocess.Popen&#96; with specific configuration for running pense commands:
+ 
+ - **&#96;start_new_session=True&#96;**: Critical for voice simulations that use async operations with semaphore-based concurrency control. Prevents FastAPI signals from interrupting child processes.
+ - **&#96;env={**os.environ}&#96;**: Passes all environment variables (API keys, configuration) to the subprocess, ensuring pense commands have access to required credentials.
+ - **&#96;stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE&#96;**: Captures output for logging and error handling.
+ - **&#96;process.communicate()&#96;**: Waits for completion and retrieves stdout/stderr.
+ 
+ This pattern is especially important for &#96;pense agent simulation&#96; which internally uses:
+ - &#96;asyncio.Semaphore&#96; for parallelization control
+ - Port management with locks to prevent conflicts
+ - Signal handling to prevent parent process interruption
+ 
+ All pense command output (stdout/stderr) is logged to backend logs for debugging and monitoring.
  
  ### AWS S3
```

</details>

</tool-use>

```diff
@@ -503,4 +503,43 @@
      return TaskCreateResponse(task_id=job_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)
  &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Subprocess Execution Pattern
+ 
+ When running pense CLI commands within background tasks:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ import os
+ import subprocess
+ 
+ # Use Popen with start_new_session=True for async operations
+ process = subprocess.Popen(
+     ["pense", "agent", "simulation", "-c", config_path, "-o", output_dir],
+     stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
+     stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
+     text=True,
+     cwd=str(output_dir),
+     start_new_session=True,  # Detach from parent process signals
+     env={**os.environ},  # Pass environment variables (API keys, config)
+ )
+ 
+ # Wait for completion and retrieve output
+ stdout, stderr = process.communicate()
+ 
+ # Log output for debugging
+ if stdout:
+     logger.info(f"Command stdout: {stdout}")
+ if stderr:
+     logger.info(f"Command stderr: {stderr}")
+ 
+ # Check return code
+ if process.returncode != 0:
+     # Handle error
+     pass
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Key Points**:
+ - &#96;start_new_session=True&#96; prevents FastAPI signals from reaching subprocess (critical for async operations)
+ - Environment variables must be explicitly passed for API keys to be available
+ - All stdout/stderr is captured and logged for observability
  
  ---
```

</details>

</tool-use>

```diff
  ## Common Patterns in Codebase
+ 
+ ### Logging Pattern
+ 
+ All pense CLI commands log their stdout and stderr to backend logs:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;python
+ result = subprocess.run(...)  # or subprocess.Popen(...).communicate()
+ 
+ if result.stdout:
+     logger.info(f"{log_prefix} stdout: {result.stdout}")
+ if result.stderr:
+     logger.info(f"{log_prefix} stderr: {result.stderr}")
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ This ensures full visibility into pense command execution for debugging and monitoring.
  
  ### Database Function Pattern
```

Updated `architecture.md` with the recent changes. Summary:

## Updates Made:

1. **Enhanced Pense CLI Section** (lines 237-249):
   - Added details on the subprocess execution pattern
   - Explained why `start_new_session=True` is critical for voice simulations with async operations
   - Documented environment variable passing and output capture
   - Noted internal async mechanisms (semaphore, port management, signal handling)

2. **Added Subprocess Execution Pattern** (lines 521-558):
   - New section under "Common Patterns" with code example
   - Documents the proper way to run pense CLI commands
   - Explains key configuration points and their rationale

3. **Added Logging Pattern** (lines 453-465):
   - New section documenting stdout/stderr logging for all pense commands
   - Ensures visibility into command execution

These updates document:
- **What changed**: Subprocess execution moved from `subprocess.run()` to `subprocess.Popen()` with proper signal handling
- **Why it changed**: Required for async operations in pense agent simulation to prevent signal interference
- **New patterns**: Subprocess execution pattern with `start_new_session=True` and explicit environment passing
- **Dependencies**: All pense commands now require proper subprocess configuration for reliable execution

The documentation now reflects the current implementation and explains the rationale behind these design decisions.
